title: Terebinthus lanuginosa
taxonomy: Terebinthus lanuginosa is classified within the family Burseraceae, which includes other tree species such as frankincense and myrrh trees. This classification is based on the plant's botanical characteristics and its evolutionary relationships with other species. The Burseraceae family belongs to the larger order Sapindales, which comprises a diverse group of flowering plants. Within the Burseraceae family, Terebinthus lanuginosa is considered a unique species due to its distinct morphology and ecological adaptations.
uses: Terebinthus lanuginosa is known to have various medicinal uses, particularly in traditional medicine. It is often used to treat respiratory issues, such as coughs and colds, due to its antitussive properties. Additionally, Terebinthus lanuginosa has been traditionally used to treat digestive issues, such as diarrhea and dyspepsia. Further research is needed to fully understand the plant's potential medicinal applications.

side_effects: Terebinthus lanuginosa, also known as the Syrian turpentine tree or the Iranian turpentine tree, has been used in traditional medicine for its antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties.
